locational.description: Characters enter a 70s-style diner and are seated at a back booth.
sentences: The restaurant he had chosen to meet at was reminiscent of a diner from the 70’s.
There was black and white checkerboard tiling on the floor, a long bar in the front, and booths and stools with red, leather upholstery.
The hostess seated them at a booth in the back corner of the diner.
The menus were laminated posters with silly names for all the foods, and since they didn’t want to read through each item description, they settled on familiar orders.
People sitting at the bar looked over and quickly averted their gaze when they figured what was happening.
The waiter appeared with the burger and reuben and set them in front of them.

locational.description: Sadie sits back in her seat.
sentences: A bell chimed as the door swung open, and Sadie entered to find Thomas, who was dreading the approaching breakup conversation he was about to start, ruminating at the bar.
Thomas stood up and gave Sadie a side hug, asking quickly, “How was your day?”
She clung onto him, “It was okay- I missed you today.”
Sadie slipped into the cushioned seat and grabbed Thomas’ hand.
“Tell me about your day!” she sang.
Sadie asked, “What’s wrong?”
After a few moments, Thomas let out a deep breath and said, “Sadie, I’ve been thinking, and I need a break from this relationship because I need to figure out my career path on my own.”
Sadie sat back in her seat.
Thomas and Sadie realized there were menus on the table, and they glanced at them quickly.
Sadie quickly asked about the reuben, and the waiter told her it was excellent.
Sadie- not so quietly- burst into tears.
I think we should stay together because we can change how much we hang out.
With the waiter gone, Sadie suddenly stood up, grabbed half of the reuben, and shook it at him as she growled, “Don’t follow me, and don’t talk to me anymore!
She stormed off, and the bell above the door jingled cheerfully as the door closed behind her.
Thomas picked at his fingernails.
Thomas continued, “Things have been really picking up at work, and I’m not sure that this is the job I want to do anymore.
“I’ll have a burger, no tomatoes,” Thomas said.
Thomas chewed on his bottom lip and picked at his now bleeding hangnail.
He grabbed his burger and took a bite of it, which created a dry and fishy taste in his mouth.

locational.description: The waiter appears and cheerily asks if they've decided what they'd like.
sentences: He looked away and addressed the hostess coming towards them, “Can we have a booth in the back?”
She shrugged and after loudly popping her bubblegum replied, “Sure, honey.”
Plus, it’s been really overwhelming between working and figuring out if I should change careers, and I really feel like I need more time to figure it out.”
The waiter appeared and cheerily asked, “Have you decided what you would like?”
Confirming that that was all they’d like, the waiter walked away.
She whispered, “Is this really what you want?
We don’t have to hang out all the time, or as much as we do now.”
“Is there anything else I can get for you two?
Will you be getting dessert tonight?” he asked.
Also, I want my polaroid camera and Beatles poster back!”
He dropped it back on his plate and wondered if the restaurant was responsible for the foul taste.
Thomas had been thinking about breaking up with her for 2 months now.
Thomas looked away and took a deep breath.
I don’t understand- things were fine, I thought.
“Oh no, we’re good, thanks,” said Thomas blankly.
Thomas sat there for awhile surprised at how quickly everything had transpired.
